versions.installHtml: Follow k0rdent Enterprise Bare Metal installation instructions
versions.verifyHtml:
kubectl get clustertemplate -n kcm-system
# NAME                           VALID
# capm3-standalone-cp-0-5-0      true
# capm3-hosted-cp-0-5-0          true
versions.deployHtml:
Standalone control plane Bare Metal cluster example: 

apiVersion: k0rdent.mirantis.com/v1beta1
kind: ClusterDeployment
metadata:
  name: capm3-example
  namespace: <NAMESPACE>
spec:
  template: capm3-standalone-cp-0-5-0
  credential: capm3-stub-credential
  dryRun: false
  config:
    clusterAnnotations: {}
    clusterLabels: {}
    clusterNetwork:
      pods:
        cidrBlocks:
          - 10.243.0.0/16
      services:
        cidrBlocks:
          - 10.95.0.0/16
    controlPlane:
      # Available since 0.4.0
      # Automated node cleaning mode: 'metadata' or 'disabled'
      automatedCleaningMode: metadata
      # Available since 0.4.0
      # Re-use the same BaremetalHosts during deprovisioning and provisioning
      nodeReuse: false
      # Default target OS image
      checksum: f0a5da9499adaaca6249792df25032430f33f0130eddf39433782b5362057b99
      image: http://<IRONIC_HTTP_ENDPOINT>:6180/images/ubuntu-24.04-server-cloudimg-20251118-amd64.img
      keepalived:
        authPass: <VRRP_PASSWORD> # optional, from 4 to 8 letters
        enabled: true
        virtualIP: <CLUSTER_API_VIP>/<SUBNET_PREFIX>  # e.g., 10.0.1.70/24. must match k0s.api.externalAddress
      preStartCommands:
        - sudo useradd -G sudo -s /bin/bash -d /home/user1 -p $(openssl passwd -1 myuserpass) user1 # define your user here. it can be used e.g. for debugging.
        #- sudo apt update # for Ubuntu
        #- sudo dnf makecache # for RedHat
        # apt/dnf commands is used here just for illustration purposes
      files:
        - path: /home/user1/.ssh/authorized_keys
          permissions: "0644"
          content: "<SSH_PUBLIC_KEY>"
    controlPlaneNumber: 1
    dataTemplate:
      metaData:
        ipAddressesFromIPPool:
          - key: provisioningIP
            name: pool-pxe
        objectNames:
          - key: name
            object: machine
          - key: local-hostname
            object: machine
          - key: local_hostname
            object: machine
        prefixesFromIPPool:
          - key: provisioningCIDR
            name: pool-pxe
      networkData:
        links:
          ethernets:
            - id: <INTERFACE_NAME>  # e.g., ens3
              type: phy
              macAddress:
                fromHostInterface: <INTERFACE_NAME>
        networks:
          ipv4:
            - id: pxe
              ipAddressFromIPPool: pool-pxe
              link: <INTERFACE_NAME>
              routes:
              - gateway:
                  fromIPPool: pool-pxe
                network: 0.0.0.0
                prefix: 0
        services:
          dns:
            - <DNS_SERVER_IP>   # e.g., 8.8.8.8
    ipPools:
      - name: pool-pxe
        pools:
          - end: <IP_POOL_END>      # e.g., 10.0.1.65
            gateway: <GATEWAY_IP>   # e.g., 10.0.1.1
            prefix: <SUBNET_PREFIX> # e.g, 24
            start: <IP_POOL_START>  # e.g., 10.0.1.61
    k0s:
      api:
        externalAddress: <CLUSTER_API_VIP>  # e.g., 10.0.1.70
      telemetry:
        enabled: false
      version: v1.34.4+k0s.0
    worker:
      # Available since 0.4.0
      # Automated node cleaning mode: 'metadata' or 'disabled'
      automatedCleaningMode: metadata
      # Available since 0.4.0
      # Re-use the same BaremetalHosts during deprovisioning and provisioning
      nodeReuse: false   
      # Default target OS image
      checksum: f0a5da9499adaaca6249792df25032430f33f0130eddf39433782b5362057b99
      image: http://<IRONIC_HTTP_ENDPOINT>:6180/images/ubuntu-24.04-server-cloudimg-20251118-amd64.img
      preStartCommands:
        - sudo useradd -G sudo -s /bin/bash -d /home/user1 -p $(openssl passwd -1 myuserpass) user1 # define your user here. it can be used e.g. for debugging.
        #- sudo apt update # for Ubuntu
        #- sudo dnf makecache # for RedHat
        # apt/dnf commands is used here just for illustration purposes
      files:
        - path: /home/user1/.ssh/authorized_keys
          permissions: "0644"
          content: "<SSH_PUBLIC_KEY>"
    workersNumber: 2

Hosted control plane Bare Metal cluster example: 

apiVersion: k0rdent.mirantis.com/v1beta1
kind: ClusterDeployment
metadata:
  name: capm3-hosted-example
  namespace: <NAMESPACE>
spec:
  template: capm3-hosted-cp-0-5-0
  credential: capm3-stub-credential
  dryRun: false
  config:
    clusterAnnotations: {}
    clusterLabels: {}
    clusterNetwork:
      pods:
        cidrBlocks:
          - 10.243.0.0/16
      services:
        cidrBlocks:
          - 10.95.0.0/16
    controlPlaneNumber: 1
    dataTemplate:
      metaData:
        ipAddressesFromIPPool:
          - key: provisioningIP
            name: pool-pxe
        objectNames:
          - key: name
            object: machine
          - key: local-hostname
            object: machine
          - key: local_hostname
            object: machine
        prefixesFromIPPool:
          - key: provisioningCIDR
            name: pool-pxe
      networkData:
        links:
          ethernets:
            - id: <INTERFACE_NAME>  # e.g., ens3
              type: phy
              macAddress:
                fromHostInterface: <INTERFACE_NAME>
        networks:
          ipv4:
            - id: pxe
              ipAddressFromIPPool: pool-pxe
              link: <INTERFACE_NAME>
              routes:
              - gateway:
                  fromIPPool: pool-pxe
                network: 0.0.0.0
                prefix: 0
        services:
          dns:
            - <DNS_SERVER_IP>   # e.g., 8.8.8.8
    ipPools:
      - name: pool-pxe
        pools:
          - end: <IP_POOL_END>      # e.g., 10.0.1.65
            gateway: <GATEWAY_IP>   # e.g., 10.0.1.1
            prefix: <SUBNET_PREFIX> # e.g, 24
            start: <IP_POOL_START>  # e.g., 10.0.1.61
    k0s:
      api:
        externalAddress: <CLUSTER_API_VIP>  # e.g., 10.0.1.70
      telemetry:
        enabled: false
      version: v1.34.4+k0s.0

  k0smotron:
    service: # Use LoadBalancer to expose Kubernetes API endpoint
      type: LoadBalancer
      apiPort: 6443
      konnectivityPort: 8132
  # Alternatively, use an ingress controller to expose Kubernetes API endpoint, see k0smotron documentation: https://docs.k0smotron.io/head/ingress-support/
  #    k0smotron:
  #      ingress:
  #        apiHost: kube-api.ingress
  #        konnectivityHost: konnectivity.ingress
  #        className: haproxy
  #        annotations:
  #          haproxy.org/ssl-passthrough: "true"
  #          ingress.kubernetes.io/ssl-passthrough: "true"   
  worker:
    # Available since 0.4.0
    # Automated node cleaning mode: 'metadata' or 'disabled'
    automatedCleaningMode: metadata
    # Available since 0.4.0
    # Re-use the same BaremetalHosts during deprovisioning and provisioning
    nodeReuse: false   
    # Default target OS image
    checksum: f0a5da9499adaaca6249792df25032430f33f0130eddf39433782b5362057b99
    image: http://<IRONIC_HTTP_ENDPOINT>:6180/images/ubuntu-24.04-server-cloudimg-20251118-amd64.img
    preStartCommands:
      - sudo useradd -G sudo -s /bin/bash -d /home/user1 -p $(openssl passwd -1 myuserpass) user1 # define your user here. it can be used e.g. for debugging.
      #- sudo apt update # for Ubuntu
      #- sudo dnf makecache # for RedHat
      # apt/dnf commands is used here just for illustration purposes
    files:
      - path: /home/user1/.ssh/authorized_keys
        permissions: "0644"
        content: "<SSH_PUBLIC_KEY>"
  workersNumber: 3
